chat._note_2026_04_28b: ADR 009 gate verdict landed — retrieval-substitution-v1-n8-k3 matches full-history quality on the conversation-eval grid (Δ ≈ 0 vs full-history; +26 pp vs naive-truncate-8). Naive truncation regresses against full-history at every aggressive window size (N=4: -53 pp, N=8: -26 pp, N=12: -21 pp; only N=16 reaches equivalent with CI kissing zero). Retrieval-substitution at recent_n=4/top_k=3 lands ~5 pp behind full-history; recent_n=8/top_k=3 effectively matches. Verdict is decisive (CIs exclude zero on both improvement gates). ADR 009 retrieval-first stance empirically validated; production-side compaction wiring is now justified work. Build plan: (1) effective_context_tokens + tokenizer_id on ModelCatalogEntry + HF tokenizer integration, (2) backend/services/compaction_service.py with vault-retrieval (not history-self-retrieval like the eval scaffold), (3) chat router wiring at per-turn boundary, (4) compaction event audit log per session, (5) system-prompt budget enforcement, (6) frontend in-context indicator + pin-turn + re-include affordances (separate chunk). Canonical baseline at tests/eval/conversations/baselines/run-20260428T112547Z.json.
chat._note_2026_04_28: Cross-link added to the latency-benchmark concept (ADR 011) — sibling harness measures user-perceived chat latency (TTFT / decode_tps / total_ms) against the same chat path under the same discipline as the conversation eval. No source changes; chat.md gained a 'Latency benchmark harness' subsection pointing at backend/tests/eval/latency/. Evening update: the eval-pinned canonical chat model swapped from qwen3:30b-a3b to qwen3:14b after the latency benchmark surfaced a chain-of-thought leak on Ollama 0.18.0 — see ADR 010 Issue 4. Chat router default in DEFAULT_MODELS['ollama'] is unaffected (already qwen3:8b on the production fallback). The static-canonical-model choice itself is being replaced by the install-time chat-model-probe per ADR 012; 'Canonical chat model' subsection added to chat.md describing the bridge state.

memory._note_2026_04_28: Cross-link added to ingest-benchmark (ADR 013) — sibling harness to ADR 011 latency-benchmark and ADR 010 conversation-eval, captures per-stage durations for the document-ingest pipeline (extract → section_detect → chunk → embed_batch → entity_extract) plus end-to-end wall clock under a paired bootstrap-CI gate. Baseline-0 (pdfplumber, 2026-04-28T124627Z) showed extract was 97% of end-to-end wall clock (19.7s of 20.3s on the 585-page 911 Report). Knob-1 landed: backend/services/ingest.py::_extract_pdf_text swapped pdfplumber → pypdfium2 (Apache-2.0/BSD-3, Python bindings to Google's PDFium); PyMuPDF disqualified on AGPL/commercial license. Baseline-2 honest measurement (apple-m5-20260428T135114Z.json) revealed harness was under-reporting end-to-end due to embed_batch_size=64 cap; with cap removed end-to-end is 43.1s (extract 0.9% / embed 98.8%), pypdfium2 swap saved ~17s (~30% e2e), not the 21× the cap-bug number suggested. Quality verified — pypdfium2 produces 2.6% more chars, 15% fewer arbitrary line breaks, identical page count and key content; on multi-column tables better than pdfplumber. Knob-2 landed: chunk-level content-hash skip in embed_note_chunks reuses binary embedding blobs for unchanged chunks, only embeds new/changed text. Production fast_ingest measurements on 911Report: first ingest 25.6s (cold), re-ingest unchanged 1.4s (18.6× faster), re-ingest with edits 2.0s (12.9× faster). Production end-to-end (25.6s) is lighter than harness measurement (43s) because production section-splits PDFs first → 3,023 chunks vs harness's 5,546-chunk whole-doc treatment; both numbers are real but measure different things.

chat-model-probe._note_2026_04_28: ADR 012 install-time chat-model self-test. Three probes per candidate model: (1) correctness — does the model honor think:false and produce a clean answer to 'say hi in one word', regex-checked against thinking-prose patterns; catches the Qwen3-30B-A3B chain-of-thought leak on Ollama 0.18.0 found by ADR 010 §Issue 4. (2) Hardware-fit — does effective_footprint_bytes(entry, default_ctx) sit under 80% of total RAM; reuses the existing helper that the future memory-pressure auto-downgrade also uses. (3) Speed — runs warm-short and chat-realistic-shallow scenarios from the latency harness once each, checks total_ms ≤ 1500 ms and decode_tps ≥ 8 (calibrated against the 14.6s-broken-vs-1.8s-snappy threshold from the M5 Pro 24 GB findings). Orchestrator iterates user-pickable catalog entries largest-first, hardware-fit prefilter is cheapest so runs first, correctness next (~5-10s per candidate), speed last (~10-20s per candidate, two scenarios); first passing candidate wins, with safe-fallback to None when nothing passes. Persistence to app/config.json under chat_model_probe key via locked_config_update; user_override field lets a user force a specific model unconditionally. 27 unit tests covering thinking-prose pattern panel (8 leaked + 7 clean cases), hardware-fit at threshold edges + KV-architecture differences, orchestrator with stubbed Ollama (largest-first iteration, safe-fallback when nothing passes, hardware-fit prefilter skips before Ollama call), persistence round-trip + non-clobbering of other config keys + override-vs-recommendation precedence. Backend service lands in this chunk; onboarding wiring (frontend) and settings re-run UI are subsequent chunks per ADR 012 migration path.

latency-benchmark._note_2026_04_28c: Amendment 3 to ADR 011: canonical baseline-0 captured against qwen3:14b on M5 Pro 24 GB / Ollama 0.18.0 / stock knobs (artifact: tests/eval/latency/baselines/apple-m5-20260428T102349Z.json). Per-scenario p50 TTFT / TPS / p95 total: warm-short 151/20.3/310; prefill-4k 163/14.5/732; prefill-16k 194/12.1/869; chat-realistic-shallow 154/14.0/1809; decode-throughput 157/13.5/21643. Sample responses verified clean (no thinking-leak prose) — 'Hi.', 'The capital of France is Paris.', actual recall of earlier-turn topic on chat-realistic. Run-to-run reproducibility ~1% vs PR-scope dry run earlier in day. Prefill is a gentle slope (TPS drops 40% from warm to 16K, vs 58% on the broken 30B-A3B). qwen3:8b cells skipped cleanly via Amendment 2's prefilter. Baseline-0 unlocks the optimization knob loop: speculative decoding (qwen3:1.7b draft, expected +50–100% TPS) is the next chunk, then flash attention / KV-cache q8 / prefix caching verify, each as its own re-run + bootstrap-CI diff against baseline-0.
latency-benchmark._note_2026_04_28b: Amendment 2 to ADR 011 fixed three harness gaps surfaced by the first 14B grid: (1) reference scenarios were duplicated per Ollama model — split scenarios in run_grid so reference runs once per grid; (2) models not pulled in Ollama errored 5× per scenario — added _probe_pulled_models(base_url) hitting GET /api/tags once at run start, absent models record a single skip_reason cell instead; (3) no per-cell sample response text in baseline JSON, forcing curl-based investigation of wrong-shape outputs — aggregate() now captures first non-errored response_text truncated to 500 chars into a new sample_response_text field. Schema additions are additive (skip_reason: Optional[str], sample_response_text: str), schema_version stays at 1. Floor test updated to allow skipped cells. Three new unit tests cover the fixes.
latency-benchmark._note_2026_04_28: ADR 011 latency benchmark harness — sibling to ADR 010's conversation eval. Captures TTFT / decode_tps / total_ms per (model, scenario, seed) cell on the actual ICP hardware (single-machine first: Apple M5 Pro 24 GB). Five v1 scenarios (warm-short, prefill-4k, prefill-16k, decode-throughput, chat-realistic-shallow) + one Anthropic reference shot for the explicit competitor benchmark. Streaming Ollama HTTP /api/chat client captures TTFT from first content delta and decode_tps from done-event eval_count/eval_duration (model-self-reported, excludes HTTP overhead). Bootstrap-CI gate on paired-cell metric diffs (LOWER_IS_BETTER for TTFT/total_ms, HIGHER_IS_BETTER for decode_tps), same statistical floor as conversations/gate.py. CLI run_bench.py supports --scope nightly|pr; PR mode is the floor-gate subset (~5-10 min) for fast checks, nightly is the full grid (~45-90 min on M5 Pro 24 GB) for canonical baseline capture. Stable-key JSON output committed to baselines/, anchored by machine_info (model_label, ram_gb, ollama_version, knob_stack); diffs across baselines surface which knob produced which effect. Opt-in floor test JARVIS_LATENCY_BENCH=1 mirrors test_baseline_floor.py discipline; skips silently until baseline-0 is captured. 28 unit tests covering scenario shape determinism, streaming-timing logic with stubbed httpx, percentile aggregation, machine-info capture, and bootstrap-CI verdict logic across all four cases (improvement/regression/equivalent/insufficient_data). No production-path changes — pure dev infra. Substrate landed in this chunk; baseline-0 capture + knob-loop chunks (flash_attention, kv_cache_q8, prefix_caching verify, speculative decoding) follow per ADR 011 migration path.

ingest-benchmark._note_2026_04_28: ADR 013 ingest-pipeline latency benchmark harness — third benchmarking surface alongside ADR 010 (conversation quality) and ADR 011 (chat-path latency). Times every stage of the document-ingest pipeline (extract → section_detect → chunk → embed_batch → entity_extract) plus end-to-end wall clock against samples/911Report.pdf. Six v1 scenarios: end-to-end + five stage-isolated. Stage-isolated scenarios share prepared upstream inputs across the warm-up + N timed runs so the measurement reflects only the target stage's variance. Reuses paired-bootstrap CI gate machinery from tests.eval.latency.gate (one statistical floor across all three benchmarking surfaces). 34 unit tests covering scenario shape, stage-dispatch correctness, timing capture, units metadata, error propagation, aggregate percentiles, stage ordering, gate semantics, missing-fixture skip behavior. Opt-in floor test JARVIS_INGEST_BENCH=1 mirrors latency floor test discipline. Canonical baseline-0 captured (apple-m5-20260428T124627Z.json on M5 Pro 24 GB / Ollama 0.18.0 / stock pipeline). Headline finding: PDF extract via pdfplumber is 97% of end-to-end on the 585-page 911 Report (19.7s of 20.3s); every other stage combined is under 600 ms. Embed-batch at 64 chunks is 500 ms (~127 chunks/s); production embed_note_chunks loops note-by-note so 5,005 chunks × single-call overhead ≈ 40s of redundant embedding cost on this document — surfaced by the harness, fix is its own chunk. Knob loop sequencing: PyMuPDF replacement first (expected 5–10× extract speedup, biggest win), then parallel page extraction (M5 Pro 12-core), then batched embeddings in production loop, then verify content-hash skip on re-ingest. No production-path changes in this chunk — pure dev infra.
ingest-benchmark._note_2026_04_28b: Amendment 1 to ADR 013: knob-1 (pypdfium2 replaces pdfplumber) landed. PyMuPDF disqualified upfront on AGPL/commercial license (incompatible with closed-source enterprise distribution); pypdfium2 (Apache-2.0/BSD-3, Python bindings to Google's PDFium) chosen as the right speed-AND-license fit. Implementation in backend/services/ingest.py::_extract_pdf_text — single-threaded per call (PDFium not thread-safe), explicit PdfPage/PdfTextPage close per page so memory stays bounded on multi-hundred-page docs. backend/requirements.txt swapped pdfplumber==0.11.9 → pypdfium2==5.7.1. Baseline-1 captured (apple-m5-20260428T131344Z.json, knob_stack=[pypdfium2_extract]): extract 19,686ms → 358ms (55×). Quality verified before swap: pypdfium2 produces 2.6% more chars, 15% fewer arbitrary line breaks, identical page count, identical key content; on multi-column tables pdfplumber was actually creating false-adjacency hybrid sentences which is worse for embeddings. Net: speed win + quality equivalent-or-better, no regression. Distribution-time obligation flagged for release-eng: ship LICENSES/LicenseRef-PdfiumThirdParty.txt in installer THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.
ingest-benchmark._note_2026_04_28c: Amendment 2 to ADR 013: harness was under-reporting end-to-end. Baseline-0 reported 20.3s and baseline-1 reported 950ms because run_end_to_end capped embedding at the first 64 chunks (embed_batch_size=64) while production's embed_note_chunks embeds ALL chunks of a note in one aembed_texts call. Fix: IngestScenario.embed_batch_size default changed 64→0 (=embed all); the isolated embed_batch(64) scenario keeps its explicit cap for per-batch throughput. Honest baseline-2 captured (apple-m5-20260428T135114Z.json, knob_stack=[pypdfium2_extract,honest_e2e_embed]): end-to-end 43,059ms p50 with breakdown extract 376ms / sections 11ms / chunk 71ms / embed_batch 42,588ms (98.8%) / entity_extract 8ms. Recalibrated knob-1 verdict: pypdfium2 saved ~17s (true baseline-0 was ~60s with all chunks embedded, baseline-2 is 43s) — ~30% end-to-end improvement, NOT 21×; the 55× extract speedup is real but extract was a smaller fraction of total than the cap-bug numbers suggested. embed_batch is now 98.8% of end-to-end and is the bottleneck for knobs 2-7.
ingest-benchmark._note_2026_04_28d: Amendment 3 to ADR 013: knob-2 (chunk-level content-hash skip on re-ingest) landed in backend/services/embedding_service.py::embed_note_chunks. Implementation: snapshot existing (content_hash → embedding_blob) rows before delete, reuse binary blob for chunks whose hash matches, only embed new/changed chunks. Vectors are bit-identical for unchanged content (raw bytes reused, not re-encoded). Five new unit tests in test_embedding_service.py cover identical-content skip, partial-edit isolation, blob bit-identity, full-replacement of obsolete rows, additive new chunks. Production fast_ingest measurements on samples/911Report.pdf (M5 Pro 24 GB): first ingest cold 25.6s; re-ingest of unchanged content 1.4s (18.6× faster, 24.2s saved); re-ingest with one paragraph edited 2.0s (12.9× faster, 23.6s saved). Surfaces the harness/production gap: harness measures compute-only end-to-end with whole-document chunking (5,546 chunks, 43s); production fast_ingest section-splits the PDF first into 60 per-section MD files then chunks each (3,023 chunks, 25.6s) — production is ~40% lighter than the harness suggests because section-split reduces total chunk count. Both numbers are real but track different things; knob iterations should track both. Two follow-ups flagged: (a) update harness end-to-end to mirror section-split path, (b) add re-ingest scenario to harness as a permanent regression-gate metric. Combined knob-1+knob-2 cumulative saving on the accidental-re-upload path: ~58s vs original pdfplumber + DELETE-and-rebuild. First-ingest of 911Report at ~26s is the new floor for this fixture on this hardware until knobs 3-7 land (ONNX threading, quantized model, smaller English-only model, background/lazy embed, MLX).

local-models._note_2026_04_28: Catalog correctness pass: fixed Qwen3 native contexts (32K not 40K/256K), Devstral native (256K not 384K), renamed Gemma 4 27B (nonexistent) to Gemma 4 26B-A4B; added Qwen3-14B, Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507, Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507, Granite 4.0 H-Micro/H-Tiny/H-Small. All entries with unverified Ollama tags carry internal=True (filtered from user picker until tags are verified against ollama.com/library) — user-pickable list is the 6 original entries with verified tags. Each internal entry carries a TODO: verify Ollama tag comment. Tool_mode taxonomy renamed: native→native_qwen3, json_fallback→adapted, limited→excluded_from_tools. Added probe_runtime_load() + GET /api/local/runtime/load — pure-Python (psutil + Ollama /api/ps), macOS branch graduates to Tauri-native after ADR 003. Catalog gained `bytes_per_kv_token` + `attention_arch` (transformer|mamba|swa, Literal-typed); `effective_footprint_bytes(entry, ctx_len)` is the helper that feeds the future memory-pressure auto-downgrade (the actual user problem: free RAM drops, model + KV cache no longer fits, swap to a smaller one rather than OOM). Rolled back this same chunk: InferenceRouter skeleton, ProfilePack 9-vertical taxonomy, slot_class field, KEEP_ALIVE_BY_SLOT policy table, route audit on every done event. Reason: the per-request classifier and multi-slot dispatch were over-engineered for the actual problem (single chat model + memory-pressure downgrade); the load-bearing pieces (catalog correctness, runtime probe, footprint helper, atomic config IO, licensing) survive. ADRs 004/005 amended with the rollback note. Cross-link added to latency-benchmark (ADR 011) — sibling harness captures TTFT / decode_tps / total_ms baselines per (model, scenario) on the actual ICP hardware (M5 Pro 24 GB first), gates future optimization knobs (flash_attention, kv_cache_q8, prefix caching, speculative decoding) under a bootstrap CI. local-models.md gained a 'Latency baseline' subsection.

licensing._note_2026_04_28: Crypto layer scaffold per ADR 006: Ed25519 sign/verify primitive, LicenseClaims schema, wire format (sig_b64.payload_b64). Per code review: feature_flags narrowed to dict[str, bool] (rejects garbage at construction time); _canonical_json constructed from explicit field references (no model_dump dependency, foreclosing pydantic-upgrade signature drift); naive expires_at rejected (not silently fixed up); cryptography pinned to ==47.0.0. 23 tests covering round-trip / expiry / tamper-rejection / malformed-input / canonical-JSON contract / timezone enforcement / feature_flags typing. Signing CLI (backend/scripts/sign_license.py) with subcommands generate-keypair / sign / verify — closes the loop on the crypto scaffold so a license can be produced end-to-end on the offline signing host. Code-review hardening (2026-04-28): distinct verify exit codes per the operator contract — 0 valid, 1 signature/tampered, 2 expired (claims still parseable for renewal UI), 3 input error (missing files, malformed JSON, wrong-length keys, refused overwrite); 17 CLI tests cover keygen perms / refuse-to-overwrite / overwrite flag / round-trip / malformed inputs / each exit-code branch. Deferred to post-ADR-003 (Tauri shell): on-disk file path resolution, OS-protected monotonic-state record (Keychain/DPAPI/libsecret), build-epoch floor, .deepfileslic UTI registration, service-layer entitlement gates. Crypto layer is platform-independent — works the same regardless of shell.
